page: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server powered by an AMD EPYC-Rome processor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.67 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The CPU allocation is shared, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations show top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU testing reaching 213580.47, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. Passmark CPU tests also show top-tier results in extended instructions, encryption, and physics simulations. However, single-threaded performance is more moderate; single-core stress-ng scores are strong at 2460.86, but single-threaded gzip compression is relatively weak at 23.32 GB/sec. Memory performance is highly bifurcated, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching a top-tier 8120.31 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths remain average at 145.72 GB/sec and 72.97 GB/sec respectively.

Given the high core count and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this server is well-suited for highly parallelized workloads such as multi-threaded decompression, database operations, and Redis key-value stores, where it achieves 101.76 million SET operations per second. It also supports moderate LLM inference tasks, processing 126.37 tokens per second for a 7B model prompt. The shared CPU allocation and lower memory-to-core ratio of 2.67 GB per core represent tradeoffs for memory-intensive applications, but the high resource density offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy parallel processing.
description: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 96 AMD EPYC-Rome vCPUs on an x86_64 architecture, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, this server delivers top-tier multi-core performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 213580.47 and strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 439.79 GB/sec. While single-threaded performance is more moderate and memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, the high core density and fast local NVMe storage provide excellent qualitative cost efficiency for parallel processing. It is highly suitable for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, Redis deployments processing up to 101.76 million SET operations per second, and moderate machine learning inference tasks.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1280 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-96c-256gb-1280s-amd features 96 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1280 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-heavy multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density 96-core AMD EPYC server with local NVMe storage, optimized for highly parallelized compute workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 96 AMD EPYC-Rome physical cores on x86_64 architecture
256.0 GB of system memory and 1280 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high-speed cached memory bandwidth
Delivers 101.76 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for multi-threaded decompression, databases, and parallel computing
